X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

ArubaClientInsight_ClientInsightSchemaGE.description:
Configuration of Client-Insight.
This feature aims at capturing L2, L3 and L4 stage on-boarding detail of the
clients. The details published by client insight feature are consumed by
HPE Aruba Networking (HPE ANW) Central to provide better
insights into client activities.

name.description: Client-insight profile name
Client-insight profile name
Client-insight profile name

ArubaClientIptrackerInterface_ClientIptr.description:
The Client IP tracking feature will learn and update the IP address of
the access devices and clients connected to the interface. This feature
is applicable to AOS-CX and AOS-S Switches.

profile.description: List of IP tracker interface profiles.

name.description: Name of the IP tracker interface profile
Name of the IP tracker interface profile
Name of the IP tracker interface profile

ArubaClientIptrackerInterface_InterfaceC.description: Grouping for Interface-level configuration parameters of client IP tracker.

client-limit-max.description:
Configure the maximum number of clients tracked on this port. It is applicable to AOS-CX switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

connect-to-network-device.description:
Marks a port as being connected with a network device. It is applicable to AOS-S switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

mode.description:
Specifies client IP tracking mode on the port of AOS-CX switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO:
Default mode. Track limited set of clients based on LLDP/CDP
signature and access/trunk configuration.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Client IP addresses are not tracked on this port.
x-enumDescriptions.ENABLE: Track all client IP addresses on this port.

mode.x-typeDescription: Specifies the client IP tracking mode of the port on AOS-CX switches.

update-interval.description:
Configure the IP address update interval in seconds. It is applicable to AOS-CX switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

ArubaClientIptrackerInterface_InterfaceC.description: Grouping for Interface-level configuration parameters of client IP tracker.

client-limit-max.description:
Configure the maximum number of clients tracked on this port. It is applicable to AOS-CX switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

connect-to-network-device.description:
Marks a port as being connected with a network device. It is applicable to AOS-S switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

mode.description:
Specifies client IP tracking mode on the port of AOS-CX switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO:
Default mode. Track limited set of clients based on LLDP/CDP
signature and access/trunk configuration.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Client IP addresses are not tracked on this port.
x-enumDescriptions.ENABLE: Track all client IP addresses on this port.

mode.x-typeDescription: Specifies the client IP tracking mode of the port on AOS-CX switches.

update-interval.description:
Configure the IP address update interval in seconds. It is applicable to AOS-CX switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

ArubaClientIptracker_ClientIptrackerSche.description:
The Client IP tracking feature will learn and update the IP address of
the access devices and clients connected to the switch. It can track
the IP addresses of directly connected clients and clients connected to
a downstream device such as a wireless access point. This feature is
applicable to AOS-CX and AOS-S Switches.

profile.description: List of IP tracker global profiles.

name.description: Name of the IP tracker global profile
Name of the IP tracker global profile
Name of the IP tracker global profile

ArubaClientIptracker_GlobalConfig.description: Grouping for Global-level configuration parameters of client IP tracker.

enable.description: Enables client IP tracking on the Switch. This is applicable to AOS-CX and AOS-S Switches.

enable-all-vlans.description: Enables client IP tracking on all the VLANs of the AOS-CX Switch.

enable-probe.description: Enable client IP probe(using ARP) to refresh the IP address of the clients. This is applicable to AOS-CX switch.

probe-delay.description:
Time delay in seconds after which clients on this port will be
ARP probed for activity in the data path on the AOS-S Switch.

trust-mode.description: Specifies the Trust mode on the AOS-S Switch.

x-enumDescriptions.TRUSTED:
Enables or disables the visibility of statically and
dynamically assigned IPv4 and IPv6 addresses for
authenticated clients on the AOS-S switch.
x-enumDescriptions.UNTRUSTED:
Enables or disables the visibility of statically and
dynamically assigned IPv4 and IPv6 addresses for
non-authenticated clients on the AOS-S switch.

trust-mode.x-typeDescription: Specifies the trust mode configuration of client IP tracking feature on the AOS-S Switch.

ArubaClientIptracker_GlobalConfigGet.description: Grouping for Global-level configuration parameters of client IP tracker.

enable.description: Enables client IP tracking on the Switch. This is applicable to AOS-CX and AOS-S Switches.

enable-all-vlans.description: Enables client IP tracking on all the VLANs of the AOS-CX Switch.

enable-probe.description: Enable client IP probe(using ARP) to refresh the IP address of the clients. This is applicable to AOS-CX switch.

probe-delay.description:
Time delay in seconds after which clients on this port will be
ARP probed for activity in the data path on the AOS-S Switch.

trust-mode.description: Specifies the Trust mode on the AOS-S Switch.

x-enumDescriptions.TRUSTED:

x-enumDescriptions.UNTRUSTED:

dynamically assigned IPv4 and IPv6 addresses for
non-authenticated clients on the AOS-S switch.

trust-mode.x-typeDescription: Specifies the trust mode configuration of client IP tracking feature on the AOS-S Switch.

ArubaDevicefingerprintingInterface_Devic.description: Device Fingerprinting Interface configuration of Aruba AOS-S and AOS-CX Switches

name.description: Name of the DFP Interface Profile
Name of the DFP Interface Profile
Name of the DFP Interface Profile

ArubaDevicefingerprintingInterface_Inter.description: Grouping for Port-level configuration parameters of Device Fingerprinting.

client-limit.description:
Specifiy Maximum number of clients allowed on the port/portlist for Device Fingerprinting.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

incoming-clients-only.description:
Enables fingerprinting for the clients that comes in after
enabling the fingerprinting feature on the port of AOS-S switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

profile-name.description:
Apply a Device Fingerprinting Profile to a port/portlist.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

ArubaDevicefingerprintingInterface_Inter.description: Grouping for Port-level configuration parameters of Device Fingerprinting.

client-limit.description:
Specifiy Maximum number of clients allowed on the port/portlist for Device Fingerprinting.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

incoming-clients-only.description:
Enables fingerprinting for the clients that comes in after
enabling the fingerprinting feature on the port of AOS-S switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

profile-name.description:
Apply a Device Fingerprinting Profile to a port/portlist.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

ArubaDevicefingerprintingProfile_Devicef.description:
To enable DFP on a port, users need to configure a DFP profile with the
set of protocol data that needs to be collected and associate it to the
desired port or set of ports. Once the profile is applied on a port,
switch will start monitoring the required packets from all the clients
on that port and collects the configured protocol and their attributes.

name.description: Name of the Device Fingerprinting profile
Name of the Device Fingerprinting profile
Name of the Device Fingerprinting profile

cdp-default.description: Enable Device Fingerprinting based on CDP TLV Platform (6).

cdp-tlv-names.description: Enable Device Fingerprinting based on CDP TLV names.

items.description: Enable Device Fingerprinting based on CDP TLV names.

cdp-tlv-numbers.description: Enable Device Fingerprinting based on CDP TLV numbers.

items.description: Enable Device Fingerprinting based on DHCP options.

dhcp-options-list.description: Enable Device Fingerprinting based on DHCP options list.

 This does not apply to Secure HTTP sessions.

ArubaDevicefingerprinting_Devicefingerpr.description:
Device fingerprinting (DFP) collects the set of LLDP/CDP TLVs, DHCP
options & HTTP user-agent details from the devices connected to the
AOS-CX or AOS-S switches. These details are used to classify the
end devices for device-type, vendor and operating systems using the
fingerprinting database.

name.description: Name of the Device Fingerprinting global profile
Name of the Device Fingerprinting global profile
Name of the Device Fingerprinting global profile

ArubaDevicefingerprinting_GlobalConfig.description: Grouping for Global-level configuration parameters of device fingerprinting.

profile-name.description: Device fingerprinting Profile reference to apply this profile across all the ports of AOS-CX and AOS-S switch.

ArubaDevicefingerprinting_GlobalConfigGe.description: Grouping for Global-level configuration parameters of device fingerprinting.

profile-name.description: Device fingerprinting Profile reference to apply this profile across all the ports of AOS-CX and AOS-S switch.

ArubaFlowTracking_FlowTrackingSchemaGET.description:
Flow Tracking is a common infrastructure to manage the IP Flow table .
 IP Flow table is used for session management of TCP/UDP/ICMP flows,
 which helps to create application aware network.

enable.description: Enable flow tracking configuration on this device.

interface-flow-limit.description:
Number of concurrent flows allowed to be in hardware on a per interface basis.
When not set, no limit is enforced.
This is only applicable to interfaces, where flow tracking is operationally enabled.

name.description: Flow-Tracking profile name to be configured to enter the flow-tracking context.

tcp-ageout.description: Flow age-out time in seconds for established TCP Flows.

tracked-protocol.description: A list of protocols to be tracked by FLow Tracking Configuration.

protocol-name.description: Protocol name for which flows needs to be tracked .

udp-ageout.description: UDP ageout time in seconds for established UDP Flows.

enable.description: Enable flow tracking configuration on this device.

interface-flow-limit.description:
Number of concurrent flows allowed to be in hardware on a per interface basis.
When not set, no limit is enforced.
This is only applicable to interfaces, where flow tracking is operationally enabled.

name.description: Flow-Tracking profile name to be configured to enter the flow-tracking context.

tcp-ageout.description: Flow age-out time in seconds for established TCP Flows.

tracked-protocol.description: A list of protocols to be tracked by FLow Tracking Configuration.

protocol-name.description: Protocol name for which flows needs to be tracked .

udp-ageout.description: UDP ageout time in seconds for established UDP Flows.

enable.description: Enable flow tracking configuration on this device.

interface-flow-limit.description:
Number of concurrent flows allowed to be in hardware on a per interface basis.
When not set, no limit is enforced.
This is only applicable to interfaces, where flow tracking is operationally enabled.

name.description: Flow-Tracking profile name to be configured to enter the flow-tracking context.

tcp-ageout.description: Flow age-out time in seconds for established TCP Flows.

tracked-protocol.description: A list of protocols to be tracked by FLow Tracking Configuration.

protocol-name.description: Protocol name for which flows needs to be tracked .

udp-ageout.description: UDP ageout time in seconds for established UDP Flows.

ArubaIpfixFlowExporter_IpfixFlowExporter.description:
IP Flow Information Export (IPFIX) flow exporter configurations. IPFIX is an embedded network flow analysis tool
that compiles characteristic and measured properties of flows and sends flow reports to internal or external
flow collectors.

exporters.description: Flow exporter list for IPFIX flow exporter Configuration.

collector-dest.description: Configure the destination type of the collector that will receive flow reports/template-packets for this flow exporter.

x-enumDescriptions.COLLECTOR_HOSTNAME_OR_IP_ADDR: Specify collector hostname or ip as collector type, which maps to hostname-or-ip-addr destination type.
x-enumDescriptions.COLLECTOR_LOCAL: Specify collector local as collector type, which maps to traffic-insight destination type.

collector-dest.x-typeDescription: Used to specify the type of destination/collector that is receiving IPFIX flow reports.

description.description: Configuration to set the description for the flow exporter.

enable.description:
GW: Enable or disable IP Flow Collector.
Default: Disabled

export-protocol.description: Configure the export protocol this flow exporter will use when sending IPFIX flow reports to the collector.

x-enumDescriptions.IPFIX: Specify IPFIX as the export protocol.

export-protocol.x-typeDescription: Export protocol to use for flow report packets.

flow-cache-size.description: GW: Determines the maximum number of entries a managed device can cache before the log is exported to the IP Flow Collector.

hostname.description: Configure the flow exporter's destination to be the hostname of the intended ipfix collector. 'ip' field must be set to null

ip.description: Configure the flow exporter's destination to be the IP of the intended IPFIX collector. 'hostname' field must be set to null

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

local-collector.description: Configure the flow exporter's destination to be the name of a Traffic Insight instance.

name.description: Configuration to set the name of a flow exporter.

observation-domain.description:
GW: Observation Domain. Allows the IP Flow Collector to group managed devices when receiving data sessions.
Default: Switch IP as 32 bit number

port.description:
AOS-CX: Configure the transport port this flow exporter will use when sending IPFIX flow reports/template-packets to the collector.
GW: Assigns the port used by the ip-flow-export-profile to export caches to the IP Flow Collector.
port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

transport-protocol.description:
AOS-CX: Configure the transport protocol this flow exporter will use when sending IPFIX flow reports/template-packets to the collector.
GW: Determines the transport Protocol this ip-flow-export-profile will use when a cache is exported.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: IP Protocol types.

upload-all-interval.description: GW: Determines the maximum time interval (in minutes) allowed before a managed device must export its cache to the IP Flow Collector.

upload-snapshot-interval.description: GW: Determines the maximum time interval (in minutes) to upload current cache snapshot.

upload-template-interval.description:
AOS-CX: Configure the interval (in seconds) this flow exporter will use when sending IPFIX flow template-packets to the collector. In AOS-CX this corresponds to the template data timeout CLI for the flow exporter.
Range: 1-86400
GW: Determines the maximum time interval (in seconds) to upload IPFIX templates.
Range: 0-86400
Default: 0 (always)

vrf.description: Configure the VRF this flow exporter will use when sending IPFIX flow reports/template-packets to the collector.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

collector-dest.description: Configure the destination type of the collector that will receive flow reports/template-packets for this flow exporter.

x-enumDescriptions.COLLECTOR_HOSTNAME_OR_IP_ADDR: Specify collector hostname or ip as collector type, which maps to hostname-or-ip-addr destination type.
x-enumDescriptions.COLLECTOR_LOCAL: Specify collector local as collector type, which maps to traffic-insight destination type.

collector-dest.x-typeDescription: Used to specify the type of destination/collector that is receiving IPFIX flow reports.

description.description: Configuration to set the description for the flow exporter.

enable.description:
GW: Enable or disable IP Flow Collector.
Default: Disabled

export-protocol.description: Configure the export protocol this flow exporter will use when sending IPFIX flow reports to the collector.

x-enumDescriptions.IPFIX: Specify IPFIX as the export protocol.

export-protocol.x-typeDescription: Export protocol to use for flow report packets.

flow-cache-size.description: GW: Determines the maximum number of entries a managed device can cache before the log is exported to the IP Flow Collector.

group-by.description: Specifies packet fields to group flows-by for top N flows.

x-enumDescriptions.EGRESS_INTERFACE_QUEUE: Group by Egress Interface and QoS Queue.
x-enumDescriptions.SRC_IP_APP_ID: Group by Source IP and Application ID.
x-enumDescriptions.SRC_IP_DST_IP: Group by Source IP and Destination IP.
x-enumDescriptions.SRC_IP_DST_PORT: Group by Source IP and Destination Port.

monitor-n-flows.description: Specify the number of top N flows to report.

monitor-name-type.description: Key combination of monitor-name and type separated by ~

reset-interval.description: The running statistics reset timeout in seconds for top N flows.

single-value-filter.description: Specifies filter applied to top N flows.

application-id.description: Application ID filter value for top N flows.

destination-ip.description: Destination IP for top N flows.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

destination-ip-prefix.description: Destination IP prefix for top N flows.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

An IPv6 prefix represented in full, shortened, or mixed
shortened format followed by a slash and CIDR mask
(0 <= mask <= 128).

destination-port.description: Destination port filter value for top N flows.
destination-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

egress-interface.description: Egress interface filter value for top N flows.

egress-queue.description: Egress queue filter value for top N flows.

ip-protocol.description: IP protocol filter value for top N flows.

oneOf.x-typeDescription: IP Protocol types.

source-ip.description: Source IP value for top N flows.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

source-ip-prefix.description: Source IP prefix value for top N flows.

An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

ArubaTrafficInsight_TrafficInsightSchema.description:
Configuration of Traffic Insight.
Traffic insight allows to monitor large amount of data that it collects from
various flow exporters like IPFIX and provides the ability to filter, aggregate
and sort the data based on user flow monitor requests.
Traffic insight tracks different monitor requests simultaneously and provides
monitor reports per request.
Enables flow monitoring on a traffic insight instance. A traffic insight instance
consists of different monitor types. Following flow monitor types are
supported:

topN-flows - Monitors IP traffic flowing through the switch and captures topN
            volume flows.

application-flows - Monitors client application flows and
                   provides application level rx/tx counters, and also brings
                   application visibility.

raw-flows - Raw-flows Flow monitor provides uni-direction flow details for
           all apps or clients to CNX on-demand basis. It is used by CNX for
           trouble-shooting work-flow.

dns-average-latency - Monitors dns request and response flows and provides average
                     dns-latency details per client.

dns-onboarding-latency - Monitors dns request and response flows and provides onboarding
                        dns-latency details per client.

workload-flows - Monitors unicast traffic flows and provides the rx/tx counters along with
                action for the flow.

flows - Monitors unicast traffic flows and provides the rx/tx counters along with
       action for the flow.

name.description: The traffic-insight instance name.

source.description: Configure traffic insight source to collect data flows.

name.description: The traffic-insight instance name.

get.description:
Configuration of Client-Insight.
This feature aims at capturing L2, L3 and L4 stage on-boarding detail of the
clients. The details published by client insight feature are consumed by
HPE Aruba Networking (HPE ANW) Central to provide better
insights into client activities.

get.description:
The Client IP tracking feature will learn and update the IP address of
the access devices and clients connected to the switch. It can track
the IP addresses of directly connected clients and clients connected to
a downstream device such as a wireless access point. This feature is
applicable to AOS-CX and AOS-S Switches.

The Client IP tracking feature will learn and update the IP address of
the access devices and clients connected to the interface. This feature
is applicable to AOS-CX and AOS-S Switches.

get.description:
Device fingerprinting (DFP) collects the set of LLDP/CDP TLVs, DHCP
options & HTTP user-agent details from the devices connected to the
AOS-CX or AOS-S switches. These details are used to classify the
end devices for device-type, vendor and operating systems using the
fingerprinting database.
Device Fingerprinting Interface configuration of Aruba AOS-S and AOS-CX Switches

get.description:
To enable DFP on a port, users need to configure a DFP profile with the
set of protocol data that needs to be collected and associate it to the
desired port or set of ports. Once the profile is applied on a port,
switch will start monitoring the required packets from all the clients
on that port and collects the configured protocol and their attributes.

get.description:
Flow Tracking is a common infrastructure to manage the IP Flow table .
 IP Flow table is used for session management of TCP/UDP/ICMP flows,
 which helps to create application aware network.

get.description:
IP Flow Information Export (IPFIX) flow exporter configurations. IPFIX is an embedded network flow analysis tool
that compiles characteristic and measured properties of flows and sends flow reports to internal or external
flow collectors.
